headline: Content Moderation Failures Leave 45,000 Harmful Posts Monthly
context: MCMC monitoring found platforms failed to remove 45,000 harmful posts per month in Malaysia during 2025.

cards.big: Platforms fail to remove 45,000 harmful posts monthly in Malaysia
cards.sub: Hate speech child exploitation and scam content persist despite reporting

cards.big: Child exploitation material comprised 8,200 of monthly unremoved posts
cards.sub: MCMC referred 2,400 cases to PDRM but only 180 resulted in arrests

cards.big: Average response time for removal was 72 hours across platforms
cards.sub: TikTok responded fastest at 48 hours while Twitter/X averaged 120 hours

cards.big: Platforms employed only 280 Malay language moderators combined
cards.sub: Insufficient local capacity means most moderation relies on automated systems

cards.big: Content moderation tests whether platform responsibility matches their reach in Malaysia
45,000 harmful posts monthly despite moderation. Platform scale makes human review insufficient; AI review is unaccountable.
